title: campanula rotundifolia f. latifolia
intro: Campanula rotundifolia f. latifolia, also known as Harebell or Bluebell of Scotland, is a flowering plant species characterized by its bell-shaped, purple-blue flowers and narrow, heart-shaped leaves. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Campanula rotundifolia f. latifolia can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusions, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
